page.content: The data presented in the accompanying table offers a comprehensive overview of the physiological metrics associated with plant samples collected from varying regions, specifically focusing on mean total leaf area (Mean TLA) and mean fresh weight (Mean FW). The Mean TLA, measured in square centimeters (cm²), serves as a critical indicator of the plants' growth potential and overall health, while the Mean FW, presented in milligrams (mg), provides insight into the biomass accumulation of the samples.

and overall health, while the Mean FW, presented in milligrams (mg), provides insight into the biomass accumulation of the

page.content: Notably, the analysis reveals a Mean TLA of 8.70 cm² with a standard deviation of 0.78, indicating relatively consistent leaf area measurements across the samples in the region of Tanga. Furthermore, the Mean FW of 223 mg, accompanied by a standard deviation of 36 mg, underscores the variation in biomass among the sampled plants. The inclusion of additional metrics such as the mean dry weight (Mean DW) and mean relative water content (Mean RWC) allows for a more nuanced understanding of plant health and adaptation strategies in response to environmental conditions. This data will be instrumental for future studies aiming to enhance agricultural practices and optimize crop yields in the region.
